text: The surgeon performs an inward T-laparotomy, revealing a tumor located in the upper part of the liver. A polycystectomy is carried out, and the hepatic pedicle is detached from the hepatic parenchyma at the hyaluronan region.
The surgeon ligates and cuts the cystic structure, taps the right hepatic pedicle, and encircles and clamps the right anterior pedicle during the liver resection procedure.

The surgeon marks the borderline of the right anterior liver segment, which has changed color. The infrahepatic part of the inferior vena cava is tapped and clamped. The Pringle's maneuver is performed, and several hanging sutures are applied.
The surgeon dissects the liver parenchyma between the left medial segment and the right anterior segment, ligating and capping small veins leading to the middle hepatic vein.

The surgeon identifies and clamps several glistening branches to segment A, causing the segment to become cyanotic. The borderline of the segment is marked, and the glistening branches are ligated and cut.

The surgeon identifies the tumor within the resecting part of the liver, ensuring an adequate cross margin. The liver parenchyma is dissected along the demarcation lines, and small veins leading to the right hepatic vein are ligated and cut. Following the resection of Segment A, a co-shaped hole is visible in the upper part of the liver.
